tps.title: error-reporting path panics because it calls unwrap()
tps.reason: Fixed with match fallback to manual JSON string

tps.title: read_token_file checks Unix file permissions but only prints a warning
tps.reason: Fixed: insecure perms now return AppError::InsecurePermissions hard error

tps.title: read_token_file converts fs::read_to_string failures into None
tps.reason: Fixed: now distinguishes NotFound (returns None) from other I/O errors (returns Err). Also fixed eager evaluation bug where file was read even when env var was set.

tps.title: client interpolates raw path and query values directly into request URLs
tps.reason: Fixed: path segments now use percent_encoding::utf8_percent_encode, query params use reqwest .query()

tps.title: chrono_offset calls duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap()
tps.reason: Fixed: now returns Result with explicit error for clock-before-epoch

tps.title: client repeats nearly identical non-success status and error-body handling
tps.reason: Fixed: extracted check_response() helper used by all request methods

tps.title: client sets a default CONTENT_TYPE header to application/json
tps.reason: Fixed: removed global Content-Type, reqwest .json() sets it automatically

tps.title: pretty-print branch uses unwrap_or_default()
tps.reason: Fixed: format_output now returns Result<String, AppError>, propagates serde errors

tps.title: compact JSON branch uses unwrap_or_default()
tps.reason: Fixed: same as above, both paths now use ?

tps.title: non-compact entity-list path builds Value::Array(entities.to_vec())
tps.reason: Fixed: format_entity_list now serializes the slice directly via serde_json::to_string_pretty(entities)

tps.title: conversion from reqwest::Error stores err.to_string() in Connection variant
tps.reason: Fixed: Connection Display now shows sanitized message, raw error stored internally only

tps.title: From<reqwest::Error> maps HTTP errors to AppError::Http with err.to_string()
tps.reason: Fixed: now uses status.canonical_reason() for sanitized HTTP error messages

tps.title: code() mapping returns AUTH_FAILED for both MissingUrl and MissingToken
tps.reason: Fixed: MissingUrl now returns CONFIG_ERROR code

tps.title: regex validation helper prepends (?i) to every user-supplied pattern
tps.reason: Fixed: safe_regex now takes case_insensitive bool parameter, callers opt in explicitly

tps.title: maximum regex length check uses pattern.len() which counts UTF-8 bytes
tps.reason: Fixed: error message now says bytes instead of characters to match the actual check

tps.title: area filter is implemented with the same matches_entity() path used for generic text pattern filter
tps.reason: Fixed: renamed --area to --name to honestly reflect it is a text match on entity_id/friendly_name, not HA area metadata (which requires websocket API)

tps.title: api_timeout parsed without range or finiteness validation
tps.reason: Added validation for finite and positive values

tps.title: Relative-time branch can overflow and panic with extreme i64 values
tps.reason: Switched to try_ constructors and checked_sub_signed

tps.title: Parser identifies relative expressions with broad contains(ago)
tps.reason: Tightened to exact 3-token structure matching

tps.title: Test module does not exercise risky branches
tps.reason: Added edge case tests for overflow, malformed ago, unsupported units

tps.title: Error impl does not expose source for Other variant
tps.reason: Added source() implementation

tps.title: No From<anyhow::Error> conversion
tps.reason: Added From impl

tps.title: Timeout errors reported with hardcoded 0.0
tps.reason: Stored timeout_secs on struct, used in error

tps.title: Non-JSON error body discarded for 4xx/5xx
tps.reason: Read raw text first, try JSON detail, fall back to raw body

tps.title: Global AtomicBool spinner interferes across instances
tps.reason: Switched to Arc<AtomicBool> per instance

tps.title: Add subcommand does not require any text content
tps.reason: Added required and num_args to clap derive

tps.title: cmd_add ignores result of create_reminder
tps.reason: Now propagates error with warning, only prints success on actual success

tps.title: Tag parsing preserves empty entries
tps.reason: Added filter for empty strings

tps.title: The test sessions_ingest_transcript_filters_credentials does not effectively verify that credentials are filtered
tps.reason: Fixed by adding body content assertions via server.received_requests()

tps.title: Threshold is not validated, allowing all findings to collapse into one
tps.reason: threshold=0 would merge everything

tps.title: Duplicate similar feedback entries can disproportionately suppress or boost findings
tps.reason: Real issue - duplicate feedback entries inflate counts. Should deduplicate by finding signature.

tps.title: Partial and Wontfix verdicts are ignored in suppression/boost logic
tps.reason: Real design gap - partial/wontfix should influence calibration decisions, not just be annotated

tps.title: Word similarity is case-sensitive, reducing match accuracy
tps.reason: SQL Injection vs sql injection would fail to match - should normalize to lowercase

tps.title: Use of block_in_place can panic on current-thread Tokio runtimes
tps.reason: Real bug - block_in_place panics on current_thread runtime. Should check runtime flavor or document requirement.

tps.title: Potential panic when truncating non-UTF-8-boundary error text
tps.reason: Real bug - &error_text[..200] panics on multi-byte char boundary. Use chars().take() instead.

tps.title: HTTP client lacks request timeout configuration
tps.reason: Real issue - no timeout means hung requests. Should add connect + request timeout.

tps.title: Case-sensitive AWS access key detection misses valid keys
tps.reason: ASIA prefix for temporary STS credentials not covered

tps.title: Overly broad key/value redaction can corrupt non-secret content
tps.reason: Real issue - auth= in URLs or prose can trigger false redaction

tps.title: URL credential redaction only handles simple usernames
tps.reason: Percent-encoded delimiters in URLs can bypass the regex

tps.title: Cache hit detection is racy and can report false positives/negatives under concurrent requests
tps.reason: Real concurrency bug - global counter compared before/after is not atomic per-request

tps.title: Feedback store load failures are silently ignored
tps.reason: Should log the error, silent degradation hides operational problems

tps.title: Internal error details are exposed to clients
tps.reason: Should return generic 500 and log details server-side

tps.title: User-controlled file path is reflected verbatim in error response
tps.reason: Could inject control characters. Should sanitize or use generic message.

tps.title: Unbounded prompt injection via raw documentation content formatting
tps.reason: Real concern - Context7 docs could contain prompt injection. Should wrap in fenced blocks.

tps.title: Out-of-bounds slice panic when start_line exceeds file length
tps.reason: Real panic - start_idx not clamped to lines.len()

tps.title: Recursive grep can escape repository root through symlinked directories
tps.reason: Real security issue - symlinks not checked during traversal

tps.title: Configuration limits are defined but never enforced
tps.reason: Real - max_iterations/max_tool_calls/max_bytes not used, agent is single-pass MVP

tps.title: Tool registry is only used for prompt text and not for actual tool execution
tps.reason: Correct - v0.5 MVP, real tool calling loop is next iteration

tps.title: Unescaped memory/project content can break hook context framing
tps.reason: Valid prompt injection vector — pipes and angle brackets in memory content could corrupt the markdown table and XML wrapper fed to LLM context. Fixed with sanitization.

tps.title: Missing validation for `chunk_size` may trigger panics or invalid chunking behavior
tps.reason: Valid — chunk_size=0 could cause division-by-zero in chunk_exchanges. Added early validation.

tps.title: No CLI-level bounds checking for chunk_size and max_exchanges
tps.reason: Fixed by adding clap value_parser with range(1..) constraints. Zero values would cause division-by-zero or empty iteration downstream. Also removed now-redundant runtime check.

tps.title: Arrow function async detection uses unsafe and inaccurate text heuristics
tps.reason: Confirmed: contains("async") on grandparent text matched variable names like asyncHandler, causing false positives. Fixed by checking for "async" child node in AST instead of text heuristics.

tps.title: Python `open()` binary-mode detection is incomplete
tps.reason: Confirmed: xb and r+b modes were missing. Fixed by expanding binary_modes array to include xb, r+b, w+b, a+b, x+b and their + variants for both quote styles.

tps.title: Function `is_in_async_function` has cyclomatic complexity 9
tps.reason: Valid. The function was overly complex due to separate text-based heuristics for each function type. After refactoring to AST-based async detection, the function is simpler with a single unified check across all function types.

tps.title: Bare-except detection uses `starts_with` on node source and is whitespace-sensitive
tps.reason: Confirmed fragile. Rewrote to use AST child iteration — walks named children for identifier/as_pattern to get exception type, and finds block child for body. Much more robust than text matching.

tps.title: Rule title overstates scope for `except Exception: pass`
tps.reason: Fixed title from "Bare except: pass silently swallows all errors" to "Catch-all except: pass silently swallows errors" and removed specific KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit claim from description.

tps.title: Tautological `.length >= 0` rule is based on text suffix instead of property access AST
tps.reason: Valid improvement. Replaced ends_with(".length") text check with member_expression kind check + child_by_field_name("property") == "length". More precise AST-based detection.

tps.title: Silent error suppression hides ast-grep execution and parsing failures
tps.reason: All 3 reviewers (GPT-5.4, Gemini 3 Pro, quorum) flagged this. Err(_) => continue hides broken rules and tool failures, making them indistinguishable from clean results.

tps.title: Rule file collection ignores `.yaml` files despite YAML extension support elsewhere
tps.reason: Real bug - users commonly use .yaml extension for rule files. Both collect_rule_files and ast_grep_has_rules need to accept both.

tps.title: No exit-status check before parsing stdout
tps.reason: Valid finding. Fixed by adding assert!(output.status.success()) before JSON parsing. PAL also caught this independently.

tps.title: Failed memory saves silently ignored
tps.reason: Valid. Fixed by adding failed counter with match instead of is_ok(). Now surfaces candidates/saved/failed in extraction result JSON. PAL also caught this.

tps.title: Signals test only checks one token from --signals and can miss partial query loss
tps.reason: Valid gap - test should assert full signals string is present in query. Low priority but real.

tps.title: Compact mode silently drops non-numeric history states when any numeric values are present
tps.reason: Fixed. Now appends non_numeric:N count when mixed states exist, so the LLM knows states were dropped from the numeric summary.

tps.title: format_numeric_summary divides by zero on empty input
tps.reason: Fixed. Added early return for empty slice: returns "samples=0".

tps.title: Watch command accepts zero interval/timeout values without validation
tps.reason: Fixed. Added clap value_parser range(1..) to both timeout and interval args.

tps.title: Fallback error output may emit invalid JSON
tps.reason: Fixed. Now escapes backslashes and quotes in error message before interpolation.

tps.title: UTF-8 unsafe truncation can panic on non-ASCII titles
tps.reason: Fixed: changed from byte-level slice &f.title[..80] to f.title.chars().take(80).collect() to handle multi-byte UTF-8 correctly.

tps.title: Model used for cost estimation is not actually the most common recent model
tps.reason: Fixed: replaced .next() with HashMap frequency counting + max_by_key to select most-frequent model from recent telemetry.

tps.title: precision_trend can loop forever when window_days is zero or negative
tps.reason: Fixed: added guard clause returning empty vec when window_days <= 0.

tps.title: UTF-8 slicing can panic when truncating memory
tps.reason: Fixed: changed &memory[..100] to memory.chars().take(100).collect() for char-safe truncation.

tps.title: UTF-8 slicing can panic when shortening the displayed id
tps.reason: Fixed: changed &id[..8.min(id.len())] to id.chars().take(8).collect() for char-safe truncation.

tps.title: truncate panics when max is 0
tps.reason: Real bug - max=0 causes underflow at max-1. Should handle gracefully.

tps.title: UTF-8 unsafe string slicing in content type abbreviation
tps.reason: Real bug - &other[..8] can panic on multi-byte UTF-8. Should use chars().take(8) instead.

tps.title: HEIC detection misclassifies any ISO-BMFF file and contains an impossible comparison
tps.reason: Correct — b"ftypheic" is 8 bytes but slice [4..8] is 4 bytes, comparison always fails. Fixed to check for ftyp box generically.

tps.title: Empty suppression patterns match every finding title
tps.reason: Valid catch — empty pattern in to_lowercase().contains() matches everything. Fixed by filtering empty patterns in parse_suppress_config.

tps.title: All non-parse file read errors are silently ignored
tps.reason: Valid — permission denied was silently swallowed. Fixed to distinguish NotFound (silent) from other errors (warn).

tps.title: Suppressed finding output is vulnerable to terminal/log injection via unsanitized finding titles
tps.reason: Valid — finding titles could contain ANSI escape sequences. Fixed by stripping control characters in format_suppressed_finding.

tps.title: Tests do not cover empty-pattern suppression behavior
tps.reason: Valid — empty pattern test was missing. Fixed by adding parse_empty_pattern_filtered_out test.

tps.title: Environment-variable detection treats empty values as enabled
tps.reason: Valid — CLAUDE_CODE="" should not trigger compact mode. Fixed with is_env_set helper that checks non-empty.

tps.title: test finding
tps.reason: confirmed

tps.title: smoke test finding
tps.reason: testing CLI feedback command

tps.title: Configured fp_suppress_count is ignored by suppression logic
tps.reason: Valid — fp_suppress_count is dead code in CalibratorConfig. Suppression uses hardcoded 1.5 threshold. Filed as future work.

tps.title: parse_verdict rejects otherwise reasonable input with surrounding whitespace
tps.reason: Valid — fixed by adding .trim() before matching.

tps.title: Zero max_review_lines produces an empty review payload and invalid notice text
tps.reason: Valid edge case — fixed by clamping to .max(1).

tps.title: Rules marked `severity: off` still produce findings
tps.reason: Real bug. Rules with severity Off should be skipped during scanning. Fixed by filtering them out before matching.

tps.title: Cache key omits max_tokens, returning incorrect cached content for different limits
tps.reason: Valid — same library+query with different max_tokens would return cached result with wrong size. Fixed by adding max_tokens to cache key tuple.

tps.title: run terminates the entire process instead of propagating contract-check failures
tps.reason: Real architectural issue. Added AppError::ContractViolation variant + exit_code() method on AppError. main now maps ContractViolation → exit 2, everything else → exit 1. eval::run now just propagates via `?`. Cleaner testability and less coupling.

tps.title: Contract check accepts non-numeric timing fields as valid
tps.reason: Real gap — field-presence check passed on null/string values, so a partially-broken server measurement path would silently feed garbage into the eval harness. Added is_number() check with a distinct error message. Added regression test for total_ms=null.

tps.title: Sweep test never verifies the query text sent to /search
tps.reason: Real test-quality gap. Mocks only matched on `rerank` and `limit`, so a bug where the harness always sent the first query would pass. Split mocks per (variant, query) with query_param("q", expected_text).

tps.title: Unknown-variant test does not prove that no HTTP call was made
tps.reason: Real gap — a regression where variant resolution happened after the first HTTP request would pass the existing assertion. Added a catch-all Mock with `.expect(0)` that panics at server drop if any /search call reached the server.

tps.title: Malformed `/search` responses are silently treated as empty result sets
tps.reason: Real correctness issue for an eval harness — silent empty cells would bias NDCG/Recall toward zero for buggy variants. Now returns InvalidResponse when `data` is missing or non-array, naming the offending (query, variant) pair. TDD-verified (RED with the old code, GREEN after fix).

tps.title: No test covers duplicate document IDs within a single cell
tps.reason: Real coverage gap. Added pool_dedupes_duplicates_within_a_single_cell — exercises the intra-cell dedup path that BTreeSet handles implicitly but wasn't asserted.

tps.title: Determinism test does not verify repeated stability of query ordering
tps.reason: Real test-strength gap. The original test had only one query so query-order stability was unobservable. Rewrote to use two queries with non-alphabetical first-seen order, and asserted both query sequence and per-query doc sequence match across repeated calls.

tps.title: Cache writes are non-atomic and can leave corrupted files behind
tps.reason: Real reliability gap. A crash mid-fs::write would leave a truncated file that subsequent `get` would reject as corrupt, failing the eval run. Replaced with write-to-sibling-tmp + rename pattern (PID-suffixed tmp to avoid concurrent-run collisions).

tps.title: Cache key hashing is ambiguous for inputs containing NUL bytes
tps.reason: Low probability but real ambiguity. Switched from NUL-separator to length-prefixed concatenation (u64-LE length per field), which is unambiguous for any byte input and locks in a stable on-disk format. Added known-vector + boundary-ambiguity tests.

tps.title: Hash test does not actually verify SHA-256 or deterministic layout
tps.reason: Real gap. Added key_hash_matches_known_vector with a fixture computed independently in Python. Any future change to hash derivation will fail this test and force an explicit decision about orphaning existing cache files.

tps.title: Search results with missing or non-string `id` fields are silently discarded
tps.reason: Same class of silent-failure as the data-not-array finding. Replaced filter_map with explicit loop that errors with (query, variant, index) context when any row has missing/non-string id. Added regression test.

tps.title: Error-path tests do not verify that invalid LLM outputs are not cached
tps.reason: Real invariant that was untested. Added judge_does_not_cache_on_parse_error — scans the cache dir after a bad-grade LLM reply and asserts it's empty. Impl was already correct (parse_grade runs before cache.put) but the guarantee is now locked in.

tps.title: Cache-miss test validates only one small fragment of the outbound request
tps.reason: Real gap — the original `body_partial_json({"temperature": 0})` would pass even if model, rubric, query, or doc were wrong. Tightened to assert model + temperature + rubric-in-system-message, plus a post-call received_requests check for query + doc content in the user prompt.

tps.title: LiteLLM judge requests have no timeout and can hang an entire eval run
tps.reason: Real reliability gap. reqwest::Client::new() has no default timeout, so a hung LiteLLM upstream would stall the whole eval. Replaced with an explicitly-built client carrying a 60s timeout.

tps.title: Default rubric test can pass even if the grading scale is missing or incorrect
tps.reason: Real weak assertion. `prompt.contains('0')` is trivially true for any prose. Strengthened to assert each grade label ('0 —' through '3 —') appears verbatim plus the "one digit" instruction that matches the strict parser — rubric drift that dropped a grade would now fail the test.

tps.title: Prompt-content assertion is too weak to catch missing per-item context
tps.reason: Real gap — `contains("excerpt")` and `contains("grade")` would pass even if the prompt dropped the query text or doc_id. Replaced with specific-value assertions: query text, each of three doc_ids, and all three distinct "Cached grade: N" lines.

tps.title: Invalid-input test can pass even if no re-prompt or validation message is emitted
tps.reason: Classic accidentally-trivial assertion. `contains("y") && contains("n")` matches the "Query:" label and any prose with those letters. Tightened to check for the specific "Please answer" / "y/n" guidance string AND the "skipping" notice that proves the re-prompt-then-skip path actually executed.

tps.title: No regression tests cover `k == 0` for DCG/NDCG/Recall edge handling
tps.reason: Real gap. k=0 is a degenerate but legal input; leaving it untested would let a future refactor that panics or returns NaN slip through. Added k_zero_returns_zero_for_all_metrics.

tps.title: Temporary cache file name can collide across concurrent writes from the same process
tps.reason: Real concurrency bug. Two concurrent put() calls on the same key in the same process shared a PID-based tmp path. The first writer's rename could remove the file out from under the second writer. Today's sweep is sequential but a parallel judge executor is on the roadmap. Fixed by replacing the PID scheme with tempfile::NamedTempFile::new_in, which uses a random suffix.

tps.title: Cache reads can observe partially written files because writes are not durably flushed before rename
tps.reason: Valid durability gap. fs::write + fs::rename makes the name visible before data blocks are necessarily synced. On crash/power-loss the final path could resolve to a zero-length file that get() then has to quarantine as corrupt. Fixed by explicitly calling sync_all() on the tmp file between write and rename.

tps.title: Function can panic on NaN inputs during bootstrap sample sorting
tps.reason: Real input-validation gap. NaN in a[] or b[] propagates to per-iteration means; the sort's partial_cmp then panics via the expect() call. Fixed by rejecting any non-finite value (NaN or ±inf) at the top of the function with a clear AppError. Added two tests: nan_in_inputs_is_an_error and infinite_inputs_are_an_error. RED→GREEN confirmed.

tps.title: Recall metric can be inflated by duplicate document IDs in a variant's ranked list
tps.reason: Real bug. Fixed by deduping each cell's ranked list by doc_id (first occurrence wins) before converting to grades. Added regression test that mounts a variant returning [d1,d1,d1,d2] and asserts recall == 1.0 exactly (was 2.0 pre-fix). Commit ee6cadf.

tps.title: Missing regression tests for legal `k == 0` edge case
tps.reason: Cheap, legitimate gap. k=0 exercises the empty-vs-empty Jaccard convention (1.0) and the short-result comparison (len < 0 is impossible so rate must be 0). Added two regression tests asserting finite output and the documented contract values. No production code change needed — tests just lock in behavior.

tps.title: Concurrent appends can corrupt the JSONL log
tps.reason: Real pattern but accepted as consistent with existing feedback.rs writer; records <4KB so POSIX O_APPEND is atomic in practice. Filed for revisit under multi-user scenarios.

tps.title: Parent directory creation failures are silently ignored
tps.reason: Fixed by propagating create_dir_all error via with_context.

tps.title: Test for missing git repo does not verify behavior
tps.reason: Fixed by testing against filesystem root instead of a tempdir that could inherit outer .git.

tps.title: Deep review tools are rooted at the process working directory instead of the fil
tps.reason: Fixed: introduced deep_tool_root(file_path) helper that delegates to pipeline::find_project_root; applied to both sequential and parallel review paths.

tps.title: Feedback verdict is accepted as an arbitrary string
tps.reason: Fixed: added clap PossibleValuesParser restricting to tp/fp/partial/wontfix at parse time; 2 RED/GREEN tests added.

tps.title: The `since` date parameter is free-form and not validated
tps.reason: Fixed: added parse_since_date validator using chrono::NaiveDate::parse_from_str to reject malformed dates at parse time; 2 RED/GREEN tests added.

tps.title: Suppression-rate zero check truncates
tps.reason: Real bug: sum of u32 values cast back to u32 wrapped at 2^32 to 0, returning 0.0 instead of the correct ratio. Fixed by computing denom in u64.

tps.title: Missing repo values are merged with a real repo named
tps.reason: Real collision: any repo literally named 'unknown' would be indistinguishable from None. Fixed by using '(no repo)' sentinel (parens are disallowed in git repo names).

tps.title: accepts `0`, which can produce invalid rolling-window behavior
tps.reason: Fixed: added parse_rolling_n validator that rejects 0 at parse time; RED/GREEN test added.

tps.title: Seed step does not assert that
tps.reason: Fixed: every seed invocation now asserts code(0); silent seed failure would have masked real test regressions.

tps.title: test does not verify repo grouping behavior
tps.reason: Fixed: now asserts a 'quorum' slice exists with exactly 5 reviews, proving grouping semantics work (fixture file is inside the quorum repo).

tps.title: Sparkline scale is corrupted by NaN/
tps.reason: Fixed: filter non-finite values before min/max fold so infinity no longer collapses finite points to level 0. Added 2 RED/GREEN tests asserting finite points produce >1 distinct glyphs even with NaN/inf present.

tps.title: trend_arrow reports invalid numeric input as flat
tps.reason: Fixed: added is_finite() guard on both endpoints; non-finite now explicitly returns flat (documented as 'unknown') instead of silently relying on NaN comparison semantics. 2 RED/GREEN tests added.

tps.title: truncate_key can panic or behave incorrectly for zero-width input
tps.reason: Fixed: handle width==0 and width==1 explicitly; width-1 underflow on 0 eliminated. 2 RED/GREEN tests added.

tps.title: Seed data depends on executing the real
tps.reason: Fixed: added env_remove(QUORUM_API_KEY) to the quorum() test helper so seed runs don't depend on developer shell env.

tps.title: By-repo test hardcodes the repository key to
tps.reason: Fixed: added current_repo_basename() helper that derives the expected key from the test's CWD .git root at runtime; forked checkouts with different names now work.

tps.title: By-caller JSON test does not verify the review counts per caller
tps.reason: Fixed: test now asserts script-a has exactly 3 reviews and script-b has exactly 2 (matching seed data).

tps.title: Configured `fp_suppress_count` is ignored; suppression threshold is hardcoded
tps.reason: Real dead code. Removed the field from CalibratorConfig, Default impl, and one test literal. Logic uses hardcoded weight thresholds (1.5 full, 1.0/0.5 soft) — the actual tunables.

tps.title: Comments still describe Wontfix as contributing to suppression after the code made it inert
tps.reason: Real stale comment left in my prior commit. Updated comments at lines 137, 195, 349, 405 to reflect wontfix is inert (retained only for trace diagnostics).

tps.title: Prompt hardening can be bypassed by unescaped code/content delimiters
tps.reason: Real vulnerability introduced when I added untrusted_code tags. User code containing literal </untrusted_code> would break out of the sandbox. Fixed by injecting a zero-width space inside the literal closing tag within the payload.

tps.title: Non-index trace records every matched precedent with similarity 1.0
tps.reason: Parallel bug to the embedding-path fix. Legacy calibrate path hardcoded similarity=1.0 instead of using finding_feedback_similarity. Fixed by recomputing per-entry similarity in the trace map.

fps.title: Changed type definitions duplicate imported names and will not compile
fps.reason: FP - CalibrationResult and CalibratorConfig ARE defined here, this IS their home module

fps.title: No validation of base_url may allow insecure or malformed endpoint configuration
fps.reason: Config::load already validates base_url with URL parsing and HTTPS enforcement

fps.title: Private key regex may fail on mixed-case or uncommon PEM labels
fps.reason: PEM standard requires uppercase BEGIN/END. Mixed-case is not valid PEM.

fps.title: Server does not enforce localhost binding despite documentation claim
fps.reason: Binding is enforced in main.rs run_daemon() which hardcodes 127.0.0.1

fps.title: Duplicate framework inputs cause redundant fetches
fps.reason: domain::detect_domain already returns deduplicated HashSet-based results

fps.title: Formatting allows Markdown/header injection through library names
fps.reason: Library names are hardcoded in framework_queries match, not user-controlled

fps.title: Duplicate AgentConfig definition
fps.reason: FP - AgentConfig is only defined here, not duplicated

fps.title: Timestamp parser is stricter than the documented/expected input format
fps.reason: Backend always returns RFC3339 timestamps with timezone. The parser matches the actual data format correctly.

fps.title: Session transcript ingestion via positional CLI argument can leak sensitive content
fps.reason: The positional arg is a file path, not the transcript content itself. Content is read from the file.

fps.title: Privacy filter not used in the ingestion path
fps.reason: strip_privacy_tags IS called in sessions.rs before ingestion (line 118). The classify functions operate on already-stripped content in the pipeline.

fps.title: Duplicate `SearchArgs` definition can cause type mismatch and maintenance issues
fps.reason: This IS the canonical definition. main.rs imports it via `use housemem::commands::search::SearchArgs`. There is no duplication.

fps.title: Function `run` has cyclomatic complexity 5
fps.reason: Complexity 5 equals threshold (not exceeds). The function is just optional parameter building — straightforward and readable.

fps.title: No validation on `limit` allows ineffective or invalid requests
fps.reason: This is a thin CLI client. Server-side validation is the right boundary. Adding client-side validation duplicates logic and creates drift risk.

fps.title: Unvalidated date/time range parameters may allow invalid searches
fps.reason: Same as limit — server validates. Thin CLI client should not duplicate server validation logic.

fps.title: Manual query string construction is error-prone
fps.reason: Values are URL-encoded via urlencoding::encode. The construction is simple, correct, and avoids adding a URL builder dependency for 5 params.

fps.title: Sensitive error details are always emitted to stderr as JSON
fps.reason: AppError variants are designed for CLI output — they contain error codes and user-facing messages, not raw request context or credentials. The JSON format is intentional for machine-parseable error handling.

fps.title: CLI help claims environment variable support but arguments are not wired to Clap env parsing
fps.reason: auth::resolve_auth intentionally handles env var resolution with multi-variable fallback (HOUSEMEM_URL, HOUSEMEM_API_URL) and TOML config file support. Clap env= can't express this logic.

fps.title: Date filter arguments are accepted as arbitrary strings without validation
fps.reason: Duplicate of search.rs finding. Thin CLI client — server validates date formats. Client-side parsing would create coupling to a specific format the server might evolve.

fps.title: Async-function detection relies on direct child kind `async`, which is grammar-fragile
fps.reason: The "async" token IS a named child in tree-sitter-typescript grammar. All 6 async tests pass including exported async, arrow, and method forms. The claim that async may be an anonymous token is incorrect for this grammar.

fps.title: Empty TypeScript catch-block detection may treat non-empty blocks as empty
fps.reason: The claim that comments may not be named children is incorrect for tree-sitter-typescript — comments ARE named nodes (extras). The filtering logic correctly handles this, as verified by 5 passing tests including comment-only and empty_statement cases.

fps.title: Wrong language mapping for JavaScript files routes JS rules to TypeScript directory
fps.reason: Intentional design - ast-grep uses the TypeScript grammar to parse JS files. All TS rules apply to JS too. Worth a comment but not a bug.

fps.title: Function `ext_to_ast_grep_lang` has cyclomatic complexity 7
fps.reason: Flat match statement mapping extensions to language names. Not actually complex - each arm is trivial.

fps.title: Unused imports from incomplete refactor
fps.reason: All imports are used by the existing 10 tests in the file. The new test added assert_cmd::Command but didn't remove any existing tests. Verified: cargo test --test context_test produces zero unused warnings.

fps.title: Removing runtime zero-check weakens defensive programming
fps.reason: The function is only reachable via clap which enforces range(1..). Adding a redundant check inside the function body is speculative — the invariant is maintained at the CLI boundary. Re-adding it would violate YAGNI since no other caller exists.

fps.title: Function `model_pricing` has cyclomatic complexity 13
fps.reason: Flat match dispatch table mapping model name prefixes to pricing tuples. No nested logic, no branching complexity. CC metric misleading for lookup tables.

fps.title: format_count rounds 999,950+ to "1000.0k" instead of promoting to "1.0M"
fps.reason: By design. The test explicitly validates (999_999, "1000.0k"). M threshold is at 1_000_000 exactly. Plan chose this boundary intentionally to avoid ambiguous rounding near thresholds.

fps.title: format_pct silently clamps invalid floating-point inputs to misleading percentages
fps.reason: Internal-only function called with known-valid ratios from analytics and stats modules. Adding validation for NaN/infinity at this layer is unnecessary — validate at system boundaries, not internal utilities.

fps.title: estimate_cost loses precision for large token counts due to u64-to-f64 conversion
fps.reason: Token counts in practice never exceed 2^53 (f64 safe integer range). A single review uses at most ~100k tokens. Even cumulative stats over years stay well under 10^12. No precision loss in any realistic scenario.

fps.title: Implementation does not match documented behavior of comparing against history
fps.reason: It compares last_changed from current state, which is correct for "what changed recently". No history API call needed.

fps.title: Untrusted names are written to terminal output without sanitization
fps.reason: friendly_name comes from HA's own entity registry, not untrusted user input. Terminal escape injection from HA data is not a realistic threat.

fps.title: Compact output is vulnerable to delimiter/control-character injection
fps.reason: Tab-delimited output consumed by LLMs, not parsed as a security-sensitive format. Data comes from HA server, not untrusted input.

fps.title: Append writes are not protected against concurrent corruption
fps.reason: Single-process tool. Telemetry is append-only from run_review which is a single-threaded sync function. No concurrent access to the file. File-level atomicity from OS append writes is sufficient.

fps.title: Malformed telemetry lines are silently discarded
fps.reason: By design, matching feedback store pattern. Telemetry file may be hand-edited or corrupted. Graceful skip is intentional and documented in the module docstring.

fps.title: Model matching uses overly broad prefixes that can misprice different SKUs
fps.reason: Model names come from user config QUORUM_MODEL env var, not untrusted input. Prefix matching is intentional to handle versioned model names (gpt-5.4-turbo, gemini-2.5-pro-latest etc). Conservative fallback pricing covers unknown models safely.

fps.title: Unknown blob formats are silently treated as JPEG
fps.reason: Intentional design — JPEG is the most common photo format and a safe default. The image crate will error if it truly can't decode, which is caught and reported.

fps.title: Blob download reads the entire object into memory without size checks
fps.reason: Internal API behind auth, blobs are user-uploaded photos capped at upload time. CLI is a local tool, not a server. No DoS vector.

fps.title: Function rule_matches has cyclomatic complexity 9
fps.reason: Complexity 9 is acceptable for a function with AND logic across 3 optional fields with early returns. The structure is clear and each branch is simple.

fps.title: Repeated lowercase allocations in rule_matches create avoidable overhead
fps.reason: Premature optimization. Suppression rules are a tiny list (typically 1-10 rules), called once per finding per review. Nanosecond savings irrelevant.

fps.title: File glob matching is always case-sensitive despite cross-platform comment
fps.reason: Case-sensitive glob matching is intentional — matches git behavior. The cross-platform comment refers to path separator normalization (backslash to forward slash), not case.

fps.title: Function should_use_compact has cyclomatic complexity 5
fps.reason: 5 env var checks is inherently 5 branches. The function is 6 lines of trivial OR logic — complexity metric is misleading here.

fps.title: Presence tests for optional JSON fields rely on substring matching
fps.reason: Substring matching for JSON field presence is the recommended approach per anti-pattern guidance — avoids brittle snapshot tests while still verifying the field exists.

fps.title: JSON omission tests use substring matching instead of structured field assertions
fps.reason: Same as above — substring absence check for skip_serializing_if is appropriate and non-brittle.

fps.title: Feedback verdict is not validated at the CLI boundary
fps.reason: Verdict IS validated — parse_verdict() is called in run_feedback_inner() and returns exit code 3 on invalid input. Finding missed the call chain.

fps.title: Suppression reason is emitted without control-character sanitization
fps.reason: The reason field comes from the user's own .quorum/suppress.toml file — not untrusted input. Finding title and category ARE sanitized (fixed earlier). Sanitizing the user's own config text is unnecessary.

fps.title: File scanning entrypoint `scan_file` is unimplemented and will panic
fps.reason: Intentional TDD RED phase stub. Function body is todo!() by design — implementation follows in step 5 of the plan.

fps.title: Core API function `ext_to_language` is unimplemented and will panic at runtime
fps.reason: Intentional TDD RED phase stub. Implementation follows in step 3 of the plan.

fps.title: Rule loading entrypoint `load_rules` is unimplemented and will panic
fps.reason: Intentional TDD RED phase stub. Implementation follows in step 4 of the plan.

fps.title: Unused imports indicate incomplete implementation and will trigger warnings
fps.reason: Imports are used in cfg(test) module and will be consumed by the non-test implementation in the next steps. Not unused — just not yet referenced outside tests.

fps.title: Rule directory traversal follows symlinks outside the intended rules roots
fps.reason: Same trust model as prior code. Rules dirs are project root (developer-controlled) and ~/.quorum (user-controlled). Symlink attack requires attacker write access to these dirs, at which point they have arbitrary code execution anyway.

fps.title: Bundled and user rules with the same ID are both loaded and executed
fps.reason: By design. Plan explicitly chose 'additive only, no override'. Users use suppress.toml to disable bundled rules.

fps.title: Filesystem enumeration errors are silently suppressed while loading rules
fps.reason: By design. Plan says 'Missing rules/ directory: return empty rule set, not an error'. Read errors for individual rules DO print eprintln warnings. Dir enumeration failures are expected for non-existent user dirs.

fps.title: Function `scan_file` has cyclomatic complexity 11
fps.reason: Standard complexity for AST traversal with rule matching and finding construction. Function is readable with clear linear flow.

fps.title: Symlinked rule directories can escape the intended rules roots
fps.reason: Same trust model as prior code. Already recorded this pattern as FP.

fps.title: Duplicate rule IDs from bundled and user directories are not deduplicated or overridden
fps.reason: By design. Plan chose additive-only, no override. Users use suppress.toml to disable bundled rules.

fps.title: Similarity matching is case-sensitive and will miss obvious precedents
fps.reason: word_jaccard already lowercases... actually checking: no it doesn't — it uses split_whitespace and trim_matches but no to_lowercase. However, the embedding-based path (calibrate_with_index) handles this via semantic similarity. The Jaccard fallback path is case-sensitive, but it's rarely used.

fps.title: `eval` subcommand is exposed but always fails unconditionally
fps.reason: Intentional for this commit — Phase 5.1 scaffolds the clap arg surface so downstream tasks (5.2+) can plug in the implementation incrementally. The error message explicitly says "scaffolded — full pipeline lands in Phase 5 Task 5.2+". Classifying as a bug would flag every scaffold commit.

fps.title: Use of `.unwrap()` may panic at runtime
fps.reason: Test code creating a tempfile helper. `.unwrap()` on NamedTempFile::new is idiomatic for tests — a failure there is a test-infra problem, not a production bug.

fps.title: Tests assume temporary-file paths are valid UTF-8
fps.reason: NamedTempFile paths on macOS/Linux CI are always UTF-8 in practice. Test code — real concern would be if run_variant_sweep itself couldn't handle non-UTF8 paths, which is a production API question not a test-assertion question.

fps.title: Cached judgments are reused even when the rubric or judge model changes
fps.reason: Misread. The EvalCacheKey hash input includes both rubric_version and judge_snapshot, so changing either produces a different cache slot and a miss. The `every_component_affects_the_key` test in eval_cache.rs proves this for all 5 components including these two.

fps.title: Cached metadata is not validated against the requested key on read
fps.reason: Defense-in-depth over sha256 — a mismatch can only happen if the cache file was manually edited or sha256 collided. The former is operator error (out of scope); the latter has cosmic odds. Adding validation would add noise without preventing realistic failure modes.

fps.title: Failed error-body reads are swallowed with an empty default string
fps.reason: Affects only the quality of an error message when the upstream status is already non-2xx. The AppError::Http still reports the status code, which is the actionable signal. Trading complexity for a rarely-useful error string is net-negative here.

fps.title: Test panics on non-UTF-8 temporary paths instead of exercising `load_rubric`
fps.reason: Test-code idiom. NamedTempFile paths on macOS/Linux CI are UTF-8. Same class as earlier UTF-8 tempfile findings — real concern would be production API, not the test helper.

fps.title: Cached judgments are reused even when the judge model or rubric changes
fps.reason: Third time quorum has raised this. EvalCacheKey includes both judge_snapshot and rubric_version; changing either produces a distinct hash and a cache miss. The `every_component_affects_the_key` test in eval_cache.rs asserts this directly for all 5 components. Recording as FP again to keep the calibrator informed.

fps.title: Use of `.unwrap()` may panic at runtime
fps.reason: Test-helper idiom. Tempfile creation `.unwrap()` inside a test body is standard Rust testing — failure means test infrastructure is broken, not that production code has a bug.

fps.title: Recall tests are logically unsound because they infer document matches from grades
fps.reason: Mismatch of test layer vs pipeline layer. The metric API is grade-based by design — `ranked_grades[i]` is the grade for whatever doc the variant put at rank i, looked up in the judged pool. Tests exercise the metric math directly; pipeline-level ID correspondence is tested by run_variant_sweep + pool_candidates. Conflating the two layers in one test would obscure both.

fps.title: Calibration may repeatedly hit cache instead of re-judging, masking nondeterminism across passes
fps.reason: Misread the code path. calibrate_judge calls call_judge_direct, which is the cache-bypass path used by audit_judgments — the cache is never read or written in that function. The EvalCacheKey is constructed only because call_judge_direct's signature takes one; call_judge_direct reads only key.query and key.doc_id for the prompt string and ignores the rest. The identical_grades_across_passes test in eval_calibration.rs stages 9 sequential wiremock responses and asserts all 9 HTTP calls happen, proving cache-bypass.

fps.title: Cache key omits document content hash, enabling stale or cross-document judge results
fps.reason: Same root cause as the cache-hit FP. The EvalCacheKey is constructed in calibrate_judge to satisfy call_judge_direct's signature, but call_judge_direct never touches the cache — it reads only key.query and key.doc_id for the prompt. doc_content_hash, judge_snapshot, and rubric_version in the calibration key path are unused. Populating a real doc_content_hash here wouldn't change behaviour because no cache read/write happens.

fps.title: Calibration computes NDCG against the same grades as both predictions and ground truth
fps.reason: The claim "every pass scores a perfect or near-perfect NDCG regardless of judge stability" is factually wrong. ndcg_at_k(grades, grades, k) measures how close the fixed rank order is to the ideal ordering of THIS pass's own grades — it is 1.0 iff the grades are already in descending order. A rank-flipping judge (e.g. grades [0,3] in rank order) gives NDCG = 3/log2(3) / 3 ≈ 0.63, not 1.0. Uniform order-preserving grade drifts (e.g. pass1=[3,2,1], pass2=[2,1,0]) correctly produce NDCG=1 each → zero variance, which IS what we want because such drift doesn't affect A/B ranking. Rank-order flips correctly produce NDCG < 1 → non-zero stddev. The test varying_grades_across_passes_produce_nonzero_stddev explicitly pins this behavior with NDCGs [1.0, 1.0, 0.631] across three passes.

fps.title: Mean metric computation can produce NaN when a variant has no per-query scores
fps.reason: Unreachable. The code guards pools.is_empty() before the push loop, and for each pool we push one entry into every variant's vector — so per_variant_ndcg[v].len() always equals pools.len() >= 1. No path that bypasses the check exists.

fps.title: Use of `.unwrap()` may panic at runtime
fps.reason: Flagged unwrap is `per_variant_ndcg.get_mut(v).unwrap()` where v iterates the same variants vec we preinserted entries for five lines above. The invariant is enforced by the immediately preceding for loop — unreachable panic.

fps.title: Overlap and short-result metrics silently ignore missing query/variant cells
fps.reason: Unreachable in current code path. compute_variant_overlap / compute_short_result_rates are only called from run_with_config, which receives cells from run_variant_sweep. run_variant_sweep fails loudly on the first HTTP error or missing data/memory field (InvalidResponse), so the input grid is always complete — one cell per (query, variant). The flagged "partial sweep" scenario doesn't exist. Same class as a prior FP on per_variant_ndcg.get_mut(v).unwrap() where the invariant is enforced by immediately-preceding code. Adding a guard would be defensive code for an already-enforced invariant (YAGNI).

fps.title: Use of `.unwrap()` may panic at runtime
fps.reason: Invariant-enforced unwrap. The flagged unwraps are per_variant_ndcg.get_mut(v).unwrap() / per_variant_recall.get_mut(v).unwrap() where `v` iterates the same variants Vec that was used to pre-populate the HashMap entries a few lines above. Unreachable panic. Calibrator already has two prior FP entries at sim=1.00 and sim=0.98 for this exact pattern.

fps.title: Function `detect_invoked_from` has cyclomatic complexity 10
fps.reason: Priority-ordered env-var checks with early returns ARE the design; refactoring to a table or loop adds indirection without reducing effective branching.

fps.title: Use of `unsafe` block
fps.reason: Rust 2024 edition requires unsafe around std::env::set_var and remove_var; this is the correct idiom.

fps.title: Use of `.unwrap()` may panic at runtime
fps.reason: Standard test idiom; panic IS the desired behavior for test setup failures.

fps.title: Caller override test does not verify the override is persisted
fps.reason: The test DOES assert rec[invoked_from] == my-ci-job on the line after the review completes.

fps.title: Use of `.unwrap()` may panic at runtime
fps.reason: serde_json::to_string_pretty on a locally-constructed serde_json::Value built from the json! macro cannot fail in practice — no cyclic refs, no custom Serialize impls, all values are strings/numbers/bools/arrays/objects.

fps.title: Use of `.unwrap()` may panic at runtime
fps.reason: Standard test idiom; panic IS the desired behavior for test setup failures.

fps.title: Duplicate feedback precedents are counted multiple times and can over-suppress or over-boost findings
fps.reason: Intended design. Each FeedbackEntry is an independent verdict. Three thumbs-up on the same pattern SHOULD weigh more than one — that's the vote tallying signal, not a bug.
